name: Small Portacabin
description:
Small Portacabin — Right-Sized for Small Teams and Tight Sites

A small portacabin from SAMAN is not a compromise — it is a correct sizing decision for buyers whose team, workflow, and site genuinely need a 10×10 or 10×15 ft cabin, not a larger one.

When Small Is the Right Size — Not a Fallback, a Fit

Five specific deployments where a small portacabin is the correct and complete solution — not undersized, not insufficient, but correctly matched to the requirement.

A single supervisor site cabin for one occupant on a construction or infrastructure project — one desk, one chair, personal use throughout a shift. A 10×10 ft cabin is exactly right. A 10×20 ft is excess floor area that costs more without adding any functional value.

A 2-person project team with no meeting requirement — two workstations running in parallel, no visitor chair, no collaborati

Compact porta cabin perfectly suited for single supervisor use on site

ve table space required. A 10×15 ft cabin provides 150 square feet that seats two people comfortably with room to store documents and move without brushing the opposite desk.

An SME additional workspace — a small business adding one or two back-office staff to a tight plot where a permanent extension is not viable. A small portacabin delivered in 7 to 10 working days is operational before a brick extension would have its foundation dug.

A compact storage-with-access unit — one person enters to retrieve, log, and return items. No sustained occupancy, no ergonomics requirement, no electrical beyond one LED and one socket. A 10×10 ft cabin with lockable door serves this exactly.

A site storekeeper cabin adjacent to material stores — document table, register shelf, one occupant monitoring material movement. 100 square feet is the right allocation. A larger cabin wastes the plot and the budget.

In every one of these deployments, a small portacabin does not leave the buyer wishing for more space — it matches the requirement precisely.

10×10 ft or 10×15 ft — The One Decision to Make

Every small portacabin buyer in India makes exactly one sizing decision. This paragraph answers it.

Clear size comparison between 10×10 ft and 10×15 ft porta cabins

10×10 ft is right when the cabin will be occupied by 1–2 people who each have a desk and do not need a visitor chair, a drawing table, or a document cabinet inside the cabin. Maximum functional working occupancy is 2 people at adjacent desks. A third person cannot be added without displacing a desk position. If the use is individual supervised work, solo monitoring, or single-person storekeeper access — 10×10 ft is complete.

10×15 ft is right when 2–4 people need to work simultaneously in the same space, or when one person needs a visitor chair opposite their desk, a drawing table alongside a workstation, a small shelf unit, or room for a standing review with a second person present. The additional 50 square feet is the difference between a functional 2-person workspace and a cramped one.

Decision rule: One person with a desk and a window — 10×10 ft. Two or more people working at the same time in the same space — 10×15 ft.

What Is Standard in a Small Portacabin — Fitout at 10×10 and 10×15 ft

Every SAMAN small portacabin leaves the factory with the following pre-fitted before dispatch from our Bangalore or Greater Noida facility.

Standard factory fitout inside a small portacabin

A 10×10 ft cabin includes: one 4-point electrical distribution board with 15A main connection provision, one AC mounting bracket with pre-wired conduit on the short wall, two aluminium sliding windows with mosquito mesh — one on each long face, one lockable steel entry door with two keys, one LED tube light on the ceiling.

A 10×15 ft cabin includes all of the above, plus one additional aluminium sliding window on the long face (three windows total) and one additional LED tube light — proportionate to the longer interior.

What is not factory-fitted in either configuration: furniture (desks, chairs, storage cabinets), AC unit, internal partition wall. These are confirmed at the order stage.

For buyers who want the full IS 2062 Grade A frame specification — steel grade, base frame dimensions, surface treatment process, and structural load capacity — our ms porta cabin page covers the complete material detail.

Small Portacabin Price and Delivery — What to Expect

A standard SAMAN 10×10 ft portacabin in MS corrugated steel with standard fitout: Rs 85,000 to Rs 1,05,000 depending on insulation specification. A 10×15 ft: Rs 1,20,000 to Rs 1,50,000.

Ready-to-dispatch porta cabin ensures quick delivery timeline

These are standard MS specification prices — not entry-level bare-minimum pricing, and not premium specification pricing. Standard IS 2062 frame, standard surface treatment, standard factory fitout.

Stock delivery for 10×10 and 10×15 ft standard configurations: 7 to 10 working days from order confirmation at our Bangalore or Greater Noida facilities. These two sizes are the most commonly held stock configurations — dispatch is faster than the standard 7 to 21 working day range that applies to larger or custom sizes.

For buyers ready to place an order — payment terms, advance schedule, and what documentation is included at delivery — our buy porta cabins page covers the complete purchase process.

When a Small Portacabin Is Not Enough — and What to Do Next

A small portacabin stops being the right choice when any of the following become true: the team grows beyond 4 people, a separate enclosed meeting area is required alongside the main working space, a drawing table and two workstations must run simultaneously, or a printer and document storage need to coexist with two active desks.

Upgrading to a porta cabin for growing team requirements

At these thresholds, the correct next size is 10×20 ft or larger — with a layout that accommodates the additional workflow without everyone working around each other. For team-count-to-cabin-size guidance from 4 people upward, our porta cabin office page covers multi-person site office configurations in detail.

If your current requirement fits a small portacabin but your team is growing and you are not certain it will still fit in 12 months — order the 10×15 ft rather than the 10×10 ft. The 50 square feet buys you the flexibility to add one more person without a second order.

For the full SAMAN porta cabin range — all sizes, materials, delivery options, and rental availability — see our porta cabins hub page.

Frequently Asked Questions

Can a 10×10 ft small portacabin be partitioned internally to create two separate spaces?

Yes, with a practical caveat. A steel stud internal partition in a 10×10 ft cabin creates two spaces of approximately 10×5 ft each — each section is usable but tight. A single-person standing-entry storage room and a single-person desk cabin is the most practical partition use. Two separate occupied workspaces in a 10×10 ft partitioned cabin is possible for very brief periods — not for full-shift occupancy by two people simultaneously. If two separate occupied spaces are needed, the 10×15 ft partitioned configuration gives approximately 10×11 ft and 10×4 ft — a functional main office and a small document or equipment alcove.

Is a 10×10 ft portacabin strong enough to be placed on a rooftop or elevated platform?

Yes, provided the rooftop or platform can carry the point load from the cabin's 4 steel skid feet. A standard SAMAN 10×10 ft MS portacabin weighs approximately 900 to 1,100 kg depending on fitout. The 4 skid feet distribute this load to 4 points. Your structural engineer must confirm the rooftop slab or platform capacity before placement. SAMAN provides the cabin's weight specification and skid foot dimensions at the order stage for submission to your structural engineer.

Can a small portacabin be used as a permanent workspace fixture — or is it only for temporary deployment?

A SAMAN small portacabin can be used permanently — it is a relocatable asset, not a temporary structure. There is no time limit on deployment. With correct surface treatment and repainting every 2 to 3 years, the IS 2062 MS frame has a 20 to 25 year structural service life. Many SAMAN small portacabins are in continuous permanent use as supervisor cabins, storekeeper stations, and SME back-office spaces for 10+ years. The distinction from a permanent brick structure is not duration but relocatability — the portacabin can be moved; a brick structure cannot.

Tell us your team size, intended use, and site location.

We will confirm the right configuration — 10×10 ft or 10×15 ft — along with standard fitout, price, and delivery timeline within 24 hours.

Call or WhatsApp: +91 97089 89937

📐 Right-sized, not undersized · 10×10 or 10×15 ft · IS 2062 MS · 7–10 day stock dispatch

Small portacabin — the correct choice for 1–4 person teams, supervisor cabins, and compact SME workspaces. Not a compromise. Factory-fitted with LED, AC bracket, windows, and lockable door before dispatch.
